All that said, this conversation goes way beyond benchmarks and touches on a number of important topics, including why we should understand Chinese company's openweight strategy not so much as an ideological commitment, but as a practical marketing tactic from companies that are seeking to gain global mind share while recognizing that Western enterprises simply can't and won't use their APIs. the culturally distinct AI use cases such as roleplay that matter in China and how these drive different fine-tuning priorities than what we typically see from Western companies, the role that Silicon Valley thought leaders play in establishing credibility for Chinese companies even in their home market.
